category_title: Students with mild disabilities exhibiting low academic engagement and passive off-task behaviors
minimal_context: The student is expected to complete a math worksheet independently at their desk during a whole-group instructional session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please take out your pencil and complete the math problems on your worksheet.

STUDENT: (Looks around the classroom without picking up the pencil or looking at the paper)

TUTOR: Go ahead and start working on the paper.

STUDENT: (Picks up the pencil but just taps it repeatedly against the desk, showing no attention to the math task)

TUTOR: You need to write down your answers.

STUDENT: (Puts the pencil down, doing nothing, and continues to look around the room while playing with their fingers)

options.A: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the math instructional content.
options.B: Pre-plan specific responses to potential non-compliance during the math instructional sessions.
options.C: Have the student create a 'Ways to Calm Down' poster to present during math sessions.
options.D: Provide individual learning opportunities for the student during the math instructional sessions.
